title: Unlocking Cost-Efficiency in Lightweight Industrial Corrugated Packaging
author: Towards Packaging
description: The global lightweight industrial corrugated packaging market is expected to grow from $40 billion in 2025 to $75.09 billion by 2035, driven by e-commerce expansion, sustainability demands, and manufacturing innovations. Asia Pacific leads the market, with China and North America experiencing significant growth. Key trends include micro-flute adoption, digital printing, and the shift toward recyclable alternatives to plastic.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Committed over $43 million to expand paper-based aseptic carton facilities, showing significant capital investment and strategic focus on sustainable, lightweight solutions.
Manufactures high-performance micro-flute containers addressing market demand for reduced material usage without sacrificing strength, well-positioned for market growth.
Supplies high-strength, lightweight containerboard grades designed to lower shipping costs and environmental impact, aligned with market growth drivers.
Offers 'Fit-to-Size' corrugated solutions and lightweighted papers eliminating excess material, addressing key market trends in weight optimization.
Introduced Performa Lumi lightweight folding boxboard in January 2026 using FiberLight technology, demonstrating recent innovation and commitment to sustainable packaging.

title: Advanced Recycled Materials (ARM) in Packaging Market Trends 2026-35
author: Towards Packaging
description: The global advanced recycled materials (ARM) in packaging market is projected to grow at a CAGR of 5.1% from 2026 to 2035, driven by stringent environmental regulations, corporate sustainability goals, and increased demand for rigid packaging. Europe currently leads the market with Germany as a key player, while Asia-Pacific, particularly China, represents the fastest-growing region. Recycled paper and cardboard dominate by material type, while corrugated and paperboard packaging lead by packaging type, with food and beverage as the largest end-use industry.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Partnering with Amcor and Licella to support construction of advanced recycling facilities in Australia, showing proactive investment in reducing virgin plastic usage and aligning with sustainability goals.
Acquired capacity from Circulus to convert plastic waste into post-consumer resins for packaging applications, demonstrating strategic positioning in the growing advanced recycled materials market.
Manufactures high-strength containerboard and corrugated products using extensive fiber recovery systems, benefiting from the growing demand for recycled paper and cardboard packaging.
Integrates advanced recycled resins into flexible films for food-grade applications, positioning itself in the fastest-growing flexible packaging segment.
Utilizes nationwide recovery network to produce high-recycled-content corrugated packaging, well-positioned in the dominant corrugated and paperboard packaging segment.
Operates closed-loop system producing corrugated solutions with over 90% recovered material, demonstrating leadership in circular economy practices within the dominant corrugated packaging segment.
Specializes in 100% paper-based, biodegradable protective packaging to replace plastic void-fill, aligning with market trends toward sustainable protective packaging solutions.

title: Stock Market Today: Dow Futures Slip, Nasdaq Gains Ahead Of Fed Decision And Jay Powell's Speech — Starbucks, Seagate, Tesla In Focus (UPDATED)
author: Rishabh Mishra
description: U.S. stock futures rose on Wednesday as markets await the Federal Reserve's interest rate decision and Chair Jerome Powell's press conference. The Fed is expected to leave rates unchanged with 97.2% probability. Key movers include Seagate jumping 10% on strong earnings, Packaging Corp falling 2% on weak results, while Starbucks, Microsoft, and Tesla prepare to report earnings. Analysts forecast a resilient 2026 economy driven by productivity gains and AI adoption, with large-cap growth stocks favored despite expected market volatility.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Up 0.33% in premarket trading, tracking the S&P 500 which gained 0.30% on Tuesday with broad market strength expected ahead of Fed decision.
Advanced 0.77% in premarket, tracking the Nasdaq 100 which gained 0.74% on Tuesday, benefiting from technology sector strength.
Down 0.76% in premarket despite maintaining a stronger price trend over short, medium, and long terms. Decline appears modest ahead of earnings announcement.
Rose 0.087% ahead of earnings with strong price trends in short and long terms plus good quality ranking. Expected to report $3.97 EPS on $80.27B revenue.
Advanced 0.23% but maintains weak price trend in short and medium terms despite strong long-term trend. Projected to report 45 cents EPS on $24.78B revenue.
Jumped 10.02% after reporting better-than-expected revenue and earnings with strong guidance. Maintains stronger price trend across all timeframes.
Tumbled 2.07% after posting weaker-than-expected Q4 results and guiding Q1 EPS below market estimates ($2.20 vs $2.26 expected).

title: Trends in U.S. Packaging Market 2026-2035
author: Towards Packaging
description: The U.S. packaging market is expected to grow at a CAGR of 4.01% from $215.32 billion in 2025 to $319.04 billion by 2035. Growth is driven by e-commerce expansion, sustainability trends, and regulatory shifts including Extended Producer Responsibility laws and PFAS restrictions. Primary packaging and paper/paperboard segments lead the market, while flexible packaging and biodegradable materials show the fastest growth rates.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Paper & paperboard segment dominates with 40% market share and is supported by strong regulatory backing for sustainable materials and domestic recycling infrastructure, benefiting fiber-based packaging producers.
Company specializes in flexible and rigid plastic packaging; flexible packaging segment is the fastest-growing format (2026-2035) driven by lightweight properties and e-commerce demand.
Focuses on labeling and RFID tracking technology; growing demand for smart packaging solutions and regulatory compliance labeling (Truth in Labeling Requirements) supports this segment.
Manufactures corrugated shipping containers; e-commerce & retail segment is the fastest-growing end-use industry, driving demand for protective and right-sized packaging solutions.
Produces paperboard folding cartons and food service items; paper/paperboard segment leads with 40% market share and strong growth in food & beverages sector (38% of market).

title: Trends in Regular Slotted Container (RSC) Market 2025-2035
author: Towards Packaging
description: The global regular slotted container market is expected to expand from $35.89 billion in 2026 to $55.77 billion by 2034, driven by rising e-commerce, sustainability demands, and cost-effective packaging needs across various industries.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Identified as a key player in a market with significant growth potential
Recognized as a major player in a market experiencing steady growth and increasing demand
